channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Does the Iran War Put America First?
item.description: How the antiwar right sees Trumpâs foreign policy.

item.title: March (Audio) Madness! Here are the finalists in NPR's College Podcast Challenge
item.description: From 75 campuses across 35 states, we've listened to hundreds of student entries to select the very best for NPR's College Podcast Challenge.

item.title: After Gen-Z Revolution, Nepal Votes for a Fresh Slate of Leaders
item.description: A millennial rapper and former mayor is aiming to lead the country, six months after the old guard was ousted.

item.title: In lawsuit, Minnesota accuses Trump administration of 'weaponizing' Medicaid funding
item.description: The federal government said the state should do more to fight fraud and is holding back funds. Minnesota officials say the attack is unfair as the sta...

item.title: House to vote on Trump's war powers. And, Minnesota sues over halted Medicaid funding
item.description: The House is set to vote today on whether to constrain President Trump's authority to continue to wage war on Iran. And, Minnesota sues the Trump admi...

item.title: China Sets Its Lowest Economic Growth Target in Decades
item.description: The goal of between 4.5 percent and 5 percent, announced at a gathering of Communist Party leaders, was the lowest since 1991 and can offer clues abou...

item.title: Refugee Sentenced to 13 Years for Stabbing at Berlin Holocaust Memorial
item.description: A German court found that the attacker, now age 20, had traveled more than 100 miles to stab a Spanish tourist, who survived.

item.title: U.S.-Israeli strikes continue across Iran; Iranian drones hit Azerbaijan
item.description: The U.S. and Israel say they conducted new strikes inside Iran overnight, targeting ballistic missile launchers. Iran claims it struck a U.S. oil tank...

item.title: Wall Street is betting on tariff refunds after Supreme Court ruling
item.description: When the Supreme Court struck down many of President Trump's tariffs, it left importers wondering how long they'd have to wait to get their money back...

item.title: Fueled by anxiety, exhausted Iranians cross the border into Turkey.
Iceland Defeats Iceland: A U.K. Supermarket Ends a Trademark Dispute
item.description: Can a company trademark the name of a country? In the case of Iceland, the British grocery store, the answer is no.

item.title: Refugee Sentenced to 13 Years for Stabbing at Berlin Holocaust Memorial
item.description: A German court found that the attacker, now age 20, had traveled more than 100 miles to stab a Spanish tourist, who survived.

item.title: As Iran Widens Retaliatory Strikes, Europe Accelerates Response for Gulf Allies
item.description: Defense Minister Guido Crosetto said that Italy would provide âair defense systems, anti-drone and antimissile systemsâ as Iran widened its retaliator...

item.title: On the Ground in Beirut
item.description: Our Beirut bureau chief, Christina Goldbaum, reports on the escalating conflict between Israel and the Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah, as Israelâs ...

item.title: Live Updates: Europe Ramps Up Response as Iranian Crisis Spills Beyond Mideast
item.description: Italy pledged air defense weapons to help Gulf nations defend against Iranâs retaliatory attacks. Iranian drones landed in Azerbaijan, a day after NAT...
